canonical_definition: NLVR2 is consistently characterized across the provided literature as a benchmark for evaluation. The prevailing usage of NLVR2 is to measure 'visual reasoning' and 'multi-image understanding', with some work also using it to assess the broader capability of 'visual understanding'. The benchmark's structure is defined by its use of 'pairs of real-world photographs' and 'natural language statements'. This operationalizes the evaluation task as reasoning over these 'paired visual inputs' to determine the veracity of a related statement. In practice, it is cited as one of several 'multi-image benchmarks' used in model evaluations, as highlighted in the paper "MIA-DPO: Multi-Image Augmented Direct Preference Optimization For Large Vision-Language Models".
papers: LLaVA-Interleave: Tackling Multi-image, Video, and 3D in Large Multimodal Models
MIA-DPO: Multi-Image Augmented Direct Preference Optimization For Large Vision-Language Models
MM1.5: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Multimodal LLM Fine-tuning
mPLUG-Owl3: Towards Long Image-Sequence Understanding in Multi-Modal Large Language Models
HEMM: Holistic Evaluation of Multimodal Foundation Models

definitions.text: An extension of the NLVR dataset that uses pairs of real-world photographs and natural language statements to test visual reasoning.
definitions.paper: HEMM: Holistic Evaluation of Multimodal Foundation Models

relationships.paper: mPLUG-Owl3: Towards Long Image-Sequence Understanding in Multi-Modal Large Language Models
relationships.evidence: First, we select five multi-image benchmarks: MMMU (Yue et al., 2024), BLINK (Fu et al., 2024), Mantis (Jiang et al., 2024), NLVR2 (Suhr et al., 2018), and MVBench (Li et al., 2024c). (Section 4.1)
